full_text:
Anastasiya Chernenko.

(See notebook 21.9.2020, 8.4.2022NC)

DNA Precipitation

Melt DNA on ice.

Add 1/10 of volume of 3M NaCl or 3M NaAc, mix well by pipetting. (7ul to 70ul)

Add 3 volumes of chilled Abs. Ethanol (210ul to 70ul). Mix well by pipetting. To chill EtOH place it to -20oC for 30min prior to use.

Leave to precipitate on ice for 15 min-1 hour.

Centrifuge 8 min at max speed, +4oC, turn Eppendorf to be backside to the rotor wall, to know where the pellet will form.

Check if pellet is visible, often – not visible.

Carefully remove supernatant1, save it in a separate tube for a while, just in case.

Wash DNA pellet with 200ul chilled 70% EtOH. Make 70% EtOH fresh, from Abs. EtOH and RNase-free water, chill 30 min at -20oC prior to use.

Centrifuge 2min, max speed, +4oC, turn Eppendorf to be backside to the rotor wall, to know where the pellet will be.

Discard supernatant2, save it in a separate tube for a while, just in case.

Open Eppendorf with the DNA pellet, put onto +50oC heating block, dry to get rid of residual EtOH, few minutes is enough (usually, 5min). DNA pellet needs to be fully dry!

Re-dissolve the pellet in smaller volume (e.g. 30ul EB or RNase-free water).

Measure DNA concentration.
